perturbation.original_text: The Mark owner may terminate the foregoing trademark license if, in its reasonable discretion, the licensee's use of the Marks tarnishes, blurs or dilutes the quality associated with the Marks or the associated goodwill and such problem is not cured within 10 days of notice of breach; alternatively, instead of terminating the license in total, the owner may specify that certain licensee uses may not contain the Marks. Title to and ownership of the owner's Marks shall remain with the owner. The licensee shall use the Marks exactly in the form provided and in conformance with any trademark usage policies. The licensee shall not take any action inconsistent with the owner's ownership of the Marks, and any benefits accruing from use of such Marks shall automatically vest in the owner. The licensee shall not form any combination marks with the other party's Marks other than the Domain Name (if applicable). If the Domain Name is deemed a combination mark, neither party shall use the Domain Name for any purpose except as expressly provided herein or attempt to register the Domain Name, and the parties will jointly cooperate on any enforcement action of infringement of the Domain Name.
perturbation.changed_text: The Mark owner may terminate the foregoing trademark license if, in its reasonable discretion, the licensee's use of the Marks tarnishes, blurs or dilutes the quality associated with the Marks or the associated goodwill and such problem is not cured within 10 days of notice of breach; alternatively, instead of terminating the license in total, the owner may specify that certain licensee uses may not contain the Marks. Title to and ownership of the owner's Marks shall remain with the owner. The licensee shall use the Marks exactly in the form provided and in conformance with any trademark usage policies. The licensee shall not take any action inconsistent with the owner's ownership of the Marks, and any benefits accruing from use of such Marks shall automatically vest in the owner. The licensee shall not form any combination marks with the other party's Marks.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than the Domain Name (if applicable). If the Domain Name is deemed a combination mark, neither party shall use the Domain Name for any purpose except as expressly provided herein or attempt to register the Domain Name, and the parties will jointly cooperate on any enforcement action of infringement of the Domain Name.', it creates an uncertainty regarding the usage, rights and enforcement of Domain Name, when a party uses a combination mark. The original clause clarifies usage of domain name, but the changed one implies the usage of domain names are limited, leaving an ambiguity on its definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Mark owner may terminate the foregoing trademark license if, in its reasonable discretion, the licensee's use of the Marks tarnishes, blurs or dilutes the quality associated with the Marks or the associated goodwill and such problem is not cured within 10 days of notice of breach; alternatively, instead of terminating the license in total, the owner may specify that certain licensee uses may not contain the Marks. Title to and ownership of the owner's Marks shall remain with the owner. The licensee shall use the Marks exactly in the form provided and in conformance with any trademark usage policies. The licensee shall not take any action inconsistent with the owner's ownership of the Marks, and any benefits accruing from use of such Marks shall automatically vest in the owner. The licensee shall not form any combination marks with the other party's Marks.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows for the possibility of a Domain Name being a combination mark and provides specific guidance on its usage, registration, and enforcement. The changed text removes this exception, broadly prohibiting any combination marks with the other party's Marks. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text (which is the original text before modification) implies terms for a scenario no longer explicitly permissible. This leads to legal uncertainty as the status and treatment of Domain Names are now ambiguous, contradicting the prior clarity.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, Boxlot acknowledges that the placement fees set forth in Exhibit A were intended to be *** in year 1 and *** in year 2, even though theglobe has agreed to extend credit terms to Boxlot by allowing Boxlot to pay over time. Thus, in the event that this Agreement terminates under Section 8.2 [Termination for Failure to Perform] due to Boxlot's failure to perform (excluding the cross-reference to Section 12.7 [Force Majeure]) prior to the end of the applicable year, Boxlot shall immediately pay to theglobe the amount necessary to pay the total year's placement fee for that year. Boxlot waives any right to assert that the foregoing fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, Boxlot acknowledges that the placement fees set forth in Exhibit A were intended to be *** in year 1 and *** in year 2, even though theglobe has agreed to extend credit terms to Boxlot by allowing Boxlot to pay over time. Thus, in the event that this Agreement terminates under Section 8.2 [Termination for Failure to Perform] due to Boxlot's failure to perform (excluding the cross-reference to Section 12.7 [Force Majeure]) prior to the end of the applicable year, Boxlot shall immediately pay to theglobe the amount necessary to pay the total year's placement fee for that year.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Boxlot waives any right to assert that the foregoing fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty.', it creates an uncertainty on wether Boxlot waives their rights and can now assert the fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty. The previous clause stated that Boxlot acknowledges the payment terms, but by removing 'Boxlot waives any right to assert that the foregoing fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty.', the enforcement on Boxlot paying the fees can now be questioned since they are not waiving their rights anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, Boxlot acknowledges that the placement fees set forth in Exhibit A were intended to be *** in year 1 and *** in year 2, even though theglobe has agreed to extend credit terms to Boxlot by allowing Boxlot to pay over time. Thus, in the event that this Agreement terminates under Section 8.2 [Termination for Failure to Perform] due to Boxlot's failure to perform (excluding the cross-reference to Section 12.7 [Force Majeure]) prior to the end of the applicable year, Boxlot shall immediately pay to theglobe the amount necessary to pay the total year's placement fee for that year.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the clause 'Boxlot waives any right to assert that the foregoing fee is a liquidated damage or a penalty' directly contradicts the enforceability of Boxlot paying the total year's placement fee. By removing the waiver, Boxlot now has the right to dispute the fee as a liquidated damage or penalty, creating a strong conflict regarding the obligations stated in the contradicted text, where Boxlot acknowledging the placement fee implied they would pay it, and waiving their rights.
